sanskrit_devanagari:
नाहं प्रकाश: सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृत: ।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥ २५ ॥
sanskrit_iast: nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasyayoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥmūḍho 'yaṁ nābhijānātiloko mām ajam avyayam

translations.verbatim_quote: I am never manifest to the foolish and unintelligent. For them I am covered by My internal potency, and therefore they do not know that I am unborn and infallible.
I am never manifest to the foolish and unintelligent. For them I am covered by My internal potency, and therefore they do not know that I am unborn and infallible.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2600 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: It may be argued that since Kṛṣṇa was visible to everyone when He was present on this earth, how can it be said that He is not manifest to everyone? But actually He was not manifest to everyone.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.25 Yoga-maya-samavrtah, being enveloped by yoga-maya-Yoga means the combination, the coming together, of the (three) gunas; that (combination) is itself maya, yoga-maya; being enveloped, i.e.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-25-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Yoga-māyā is the combination (yoga) of the three guṇas which is itself the veiling power (māyā). Krishna, enveloped by yoga-māyā, is not manifest to all but only to certain devotees. The deluded world, accordingly, does not know him as ajam (unborn) and avyayam (imperishable).

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.25 Concealed by the Maya called Yogamaya, I am associated with a human form and other generic structures which are special to individual selves. Because of this I am not manifest to all.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-25-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Concealed by yoga-māyā, Krishna is associated with the human form and other generic structures special to individual selves. Because of this he is not manifest to all. The foolish see only the human form and do not know that his powers exceed those of Vāyu and Indra, that his lustre is more brilliant than sun and fire, and that he is unborn, immutable, the cause of all worlds.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All readings agree that yoga-māyā is what conceals Krishna's reality from the deluded world. Shankara analyses it as the guṇa-combination that veils; Ramanuja analyses it as the divine-māyā that wraps Krishna in human/generic form so the surface appears ordinary. The engineering analog is heavily STRETCHED: the verse claims a metaphysical concealment, while the engineering layer can only describe the operational surface that obscures substrate. The two are not the same thing.

literal_meaning: I am not manifest to all, veiled by yoga-māyā; this deluded world does not know me — unborn, imperishable.
traditional_meaning_consensus: nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ / mūḍho 'yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam — Krishna is not manifest (na prakāśaḥ) to all; he is concealed (samāvṛtaḥ) by yoga-māyā; this deluded world (mūḍho 'yaṁ lokaḥ) does not know him as unborn (ajam), imperishable (avyayam). Shankara: yoga-māyā = the combination of the three guṇas that veils Krishna's reality; he becomes manifest only to certain devotees; the world, deluded, does not know him. Ramanuja: yoga-māyā wraps Krishna in human form and other generic structures; the foolish, seeing only the human form, do not know that his powers exceed those of Vāyu and Indra, that his lustre exceeds sun and fire, that he is unborn, immutable, the cause of all worlds.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
